item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Title: 48 Cancri.jpg
Author: Egres73
Release year: 2013

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wonder if anyone here has actually made an scp any related to Kirby besides what I'm working on.

You might be interested in this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, upset that his parents are there at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first glance at the title I thought it said Moonstruck Blossom. Bit disappointed it wasn't what it actually said, but now I wonder if anyone here has actually made an scp any related to Kirby besides what I'm working on.
I wonder what this kid in space is up to. He seems upset that people are up there with him now. Hmmmmm

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's good,I like this one

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In 1971 I was 13 years old, lived in a Cleveland suburb, and was obsessed with Astronomy.
This could almost be me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BVn1oQL9sWg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic. The bit about the astrology books made me giggle, and I overall love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: /me preens. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this version.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: and then a skeleton popped out

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ^This is also what compelled me to upvote. Pseudoscience can go fuck itself. >:)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does that mean you wrote the braaaaaaaaaaains?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, I helped with the concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Astrology books will be severely damaged by fire

Yes. YES. >=D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He wrote the skeleton, I wrote the flesh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Roget, you're a goddamn machine. Seriously, if I put one of my drafts through 914 on "very fine", an article of yours would pop out.

+1ed. This is delightfully original and creative. Never stop writin' skips, and my gift card offer for your 100th still stands. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The second effect took me by surprise, and overall this is a solid SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, gotcha.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To clarify:

people who are legally blind may not be totally blind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: People who aren't totally blind. If you have a blindfold, are legally blind, or only blind in one eye, you can still see to some degree.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really don't understand the "any sighted human" part even with the footnote.

Do you mean any human that can see? Based on the footnote I would say no, but then I have no good explanation.

Do you mean any human that are seen?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two minutes after an activation event is triggered, an unidentified flying object can be detected approximately 2,400 km above the earth, moving away at approximately 33 km/s.

1) Wouldn't experimentation have been forbidden, on the grounds that astronomers might notice something in space appearing from out of nowhere?

2) Given that they did experiment, why didn't they try putting beacons on the test subjects to see if the beacons showed up in the UFOs?

item.title: YES
item.content:encoded: I loved this one. It was simple, with a child achieving his dream despite the naysayers (and logic). The current one has the same idea, but is too convoluted for me to have any emotional reaction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I gotta say, this feels like two SCPs, and I think the "kid's room that launches people into space" is way more interesting than yet another compulsion effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the earliest draft I could find
if it isn't this one you were thinking of something else
SCP-1683 interior.

Item #: SCP-1683

Object Class: Safe

Special Containment Procedures: The entrance to SCP-1683 is to be sealed and the interior monitored by security personnel. No personnel above D-Class are permitted into SCP-1683's interior. A containment site has been set up in the residence connected to SCP-1683, with several other homes in the neighborhood having been purchased in order to create a buffer between civilian residences and SCP-1683. A cover story regarding ongoing construction work has been implemented, pending further developments in SCP-1683's containment method.

Description: SCP-1683 is a children's bedroom, located on the second floor of a two-story residential home. SCP-1683 contains one furnished bed, a dresser, one telescope, a ceiling covered in luminescent space-themed stickers, and several pieces of media regarding space travel. Notably, the residence's second floor contains no windows.

Subjects entering SCP-1683 will note the realism and detail put into the stickers, which are all located on the ceiling of SCP-1683. Analysis has shown that they accurately map many known star systems and planetary locations. As the subject continues to observe the stickers, they will begin to accurately mimic the current appearance of the night sky, although satellites and other airborne objects will not be visible.

When a subject interacts with the telescope located within SCP-1683, they will claim to see the constellations, and be able to name them despite any previous knowledge. Once all currently visible constellations have been identified, the entrance to the room will become sealed from the inside, and impossible to open. Attempting to enter SCP-1683 via alternate means has not been attempted due to the possibly irreversible damage to SCP-1683.

After twenty minutes, it will become possible to enter SCP-1683. There will be no sign of any subjects previously occupying it. At the same time, orbiting satellites will report a sudden human-shaped anomaly in space appearing approximately 2000 kilometers over the surface of the earth, heading into deep space. Analysis has shown that the subjects appear to be identical to those who entered SCP-1683.

SCP-1683 was discovered in 1971, following a set of police reports detailing SCP-1683's effect. The subject originally residing within SCP-1683, a young adult male, had gone missing directly prior to the first recorded manifestation of SCP-1683's effect. The subjects parents described him as having a deep interest in space exploration, which they had discouraged due to his isolation from peers. Neighbors reported rarely seeing the subject outside of his home, and some were unaware of his existence. It is currently believed that the subject initiated SCP-1683's effect, although how he did so is unknown.

As of 8/19/1976, SCP-1683 has been classified as Safe.

Addendum: Documentation recovered from classified U.S and Russian Federation records indicate that on 12/25/1969, satellites indicated that a small humanoid, appearing to be wearing some form of spacesuit, suddenly manifested and began rapidly moving away from the earth's surface. This was followed by several other unknown objects, believed to be supplies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope, even before that one. The one you showed in chat a while back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this is the version he is referring to
hide
Portion of SCP-1683's interior.

Item #: SCP-1683

Object Class: Safe

Special Containment Procedures: The entrance to SCP-1683 is to be sealed and the interior monitored by security personnel. Only D-Class personnel are permitted into SCP-1683's interior. A containment site has been set up in the residence connected to SCP-1683, with several other homes in the neighborhood having been purchased in order to facilitate containment. A cover story regarding ongoing construction work has been implemented, pending further developments in SCP-1683's containment method.

During activation events, all Foundation personnel are required to wear ear and eye protection, and not to come within 20 meters of SCP-1683. In the event that a member of site personnel suffers a hearing injury, the inability to follow proper procedure shall be noted on their disciplinary record.

Description: SCP-1683 is a child's bedroom, located on the second floor of a two-story residential home in . SCP-1683 contains one furnished bed, a dresser, one telescope, a ceiling covered in luminescent outer-space-themed stickers, and several posters and books regarding space exploration. Notably, the residence's second floor contains no windows. Analysis has shown that they accurately map many known star systems and planetary locations, although only ones known when SCP-1683 was created.

Subjects who enter SCP-1683 will immediately and permanently have a fascination with astronomy. Testing has shown that one D-Class personnel exposed to SCP-1683 was affected for a full year, prior to termination. Typically, subjects will begin to observe the stickers within SCP-1683, and compare them to the books. As the subject continues to observe, the stickers will begin to accurately mimic the current appearance of the night sky, with satellites and other man-made objects not being visible.

Sighted subjects, including those who are legally blind or blind in one eye, will be affected if they contact the telescope. If the subject makes skin contact with the telescope, the door will shut and entry will become impossible. While the door is sealed, the sound of a human subject counting down from ten to one can be heard. This voice will resemble the subject's voice, although in mute subjects the countdown will be identical to recordings of the Apollo 13 launch. Attempts to record what occurs within SCP-1683 results in all footage becoming identical to the television broadcast of the Apollo 13 launch.

Following this, the walls and door around SCP-1683 will rapidly be heated to 3300 degrees Celsius, although no ignition will occur, while sound identical to the Apollo 13 liftoff can be heard. These sounds will not be heard outside of the house SCP-1683 is located in. Notably, astronomy books made after 1971, and any other genre of book made in any time period are unaffected by SCP-1683. Astronomy books from this period will vanish with the subject. Books on astrology, or fiction using "soft" science will be incinerated.

After twenty minutes, it will become possible to enter SCP-1683. There will be no sign of any subjects previously occupying it. At the same time, orbiting satellites will report a human-shaped anomaly in space appearing approximately 2000 kilometers over the surface of the earth, rapidly moving away. Analysis has shown that the objects resemble subjects who disappeared within SCP-1683.

SCP-1683 was discovered in 1971, following a set of police reports detailing SCP-1683's effect. The subjects originally residing within SCP-1683, family with two parents and one male child, had gone missing directly prior to the first recorded manifestation of SCP-1683's effect. Neighbors described the son as having a deep interest in space exploration, spending most of his time studying it. It is currently believed that this subject initiated SCP-1683's effect, although how he did so is unknown. All witnesses have been issued Class-C to -B amnestics, depending on their relationship with the subjects.

As of 8/19/1976, SCP-1683 has been classified as Safe.

Addendum: When SCP-1683 was searched, a document appearing to chart a route from earth to the moon was discovered within a book. This document was heavily worn with eraser marks and changes, indicating that it had been significantly altered several times prior to being discovered. In addition, this writing was discovered on the opposite side.

If there's been a giant leap for mankind, why am I still wasting time here? Tomorrow is waiting, and everything is set. Scopes are set up and the stars are there, watching. I'll see them soon

Addendum: On 09/18/2008, Foundation deep-space satellites orbiting Saturn received a transmission, believed to be related to SCP-1683.

Hello? Can someone hear this? I need you to take them back. You've been sending the others, right? If you know how it works, or if you've changed it or anything like that, I need you to please take them-

(2 minutes of transmission are indecipherable)


-Neptune won't even take them in. They're not dreamers, and they don't belong here. I know some of your people have been doing well, those are fine. I got lonely here by myself. But my parents have no place here. Take them, before they hurt themselves again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was the first draft more heartwarming?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: :/ I actually liked the first draft a lot more. Neutral vote because it's still interesting, just not heartwarming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well I'll be damned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can just mouse over the footnote number, and it'll pop up right there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, I really like this one. I geniunely had no idea where this was going until the actual launch.

I would like a line in there somewhere about attempting to track/follow the UFO as it leaves. Seems like an interesting avenue of research on this one that isn't mentioned anywhere in the article.

I'm not sure how I feel about all the superscripts. I get the appeal, of including relevant data without cluttering the article, but the result was that I kept having to scroll back and forth between the two. Kind of annoying. EDIT: I'm dumb.

Overall, however, great SCP. I actually kind of chuckled, though the addendum did a nice job of supressing the warm fuzzies I got at the idea of a kid successfully making his own magical rocket.

Also, I have been playing KSP non-stop for the past week, so it earned brownie points there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SPAEC

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Original idea came from a discussion with JR in the chat, and eventually became a collaboration with me and Voct. Woo!

I'll be uploading the picture in a jiffy!
